dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Most people dream of a normal 9-to-5. A desk job, maybe. A quiet office. Me? I dream of a 9-to-5 where my coworkers don't sparkle in the sun and my customers don't eye my neck like it's a five-star buffet.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Welcome to Nocturne Cafe. Where the 'iced coffee' is actually blood, and the only thing colder than the drinks are the stares from our patrons.

dialogue.speaker: Eve
dialogue.line: Order up! One 'Midnight Bloom' for table seven! And a 'Crimson Dew' for... oh, Mr. Elias!

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: CLINK (of glasses), WHIRR (espresso machine), WHOOSH (steam)

dialogue.speaker: Eve
dialogue.line: Oops! Almost forgot your usual, sir.

dialogue.speaker: Eve
dialogue.line: Okay, Eve, deep breaths. It's just another Tuesday. A Tuesday filled with immortal beings who find human clumsiness... endearing? Terrifying? I can never tell.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: My name is Eve. And I'm the only human barista at Nocturne Cafe. Which means I'm constantly on edge, constantly trying not to spill, break, or accidentally offer a garlic breadstick to a creature of the night.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: THUD (Eve bumps into counter), SPLASH (drink sloshes)

dialogue.speaker: Eve
dialogue.line: Agh! My apologies!

dialogue.speaker: Elias
dialogue.line: Careful, little human. You wouldn't want to waste such a... vibrant shade.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: He was there. Standing by the counter, as if he'd materialized from the shadows themselves. Elias. The Patron. Always impeccably dressed, always with that look in his eyes that seemed to see right through you.

dialogue.speaker: Eve
dialogue.line: Mr. Elias! Good evening! Your 'Crimson Dew' is almost ready!

dialogue.speaker: Eve
dialogue.line: Oh no, not him. He always looks like he's judging my life choices. Or my blood type. And he’s so… perfect. Too perfect. His hair, his eyes, the way he just *exists* in a room. It's unfair. And he's a vampire. A very, very handsome vampire.

dialogue.speaker: Elias
dialogue.line: There's no rush. I find your... enthusiasm... quite entertaining.

dialogue.speaker: Eve
dialogue.line: Entertaining? Is that a compliment? A threat? A philosophical musing? With vampires, it's always a guessing game.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: CLINK (ice in glass)

dialogue.speaker: Eve
dialogue.line: Here you are, Mr. Elias. Freshly... drawn.

dialogue.speaker: Elias
dialogue.line: Thank you, Eve.

dialogue.speaker: Eve
dialogue.line: He remembered my name! My heart just did a little human flutter. Get it together, Eve. He's a predator. A very charming predator.

dialogue.speaker: Elias
dialogue.line: Tell me, Eve. What is it that draws a human such as yourself to a place like this?

dialogue.speaker: Eve
dialogue.line: Well... the pay is good? And... it's never boring?

dialogue.speaker: Elias
dialogue.line: Never boring. Indeed. Perhaps I understand your fascination with the mundane, then.

dialogue.speaker: Eve
dialogue.line: Mundane? My life is anything but mundane here!

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The cafe emptied out as the first hints of dawn painted the sky. For most, that's the start of the day. For us, it's the end of the night shift. And for me, it's another night survived.

dialogue.speaker: Eve
dialogue.line: No bites, no major spills, and I even managed to make Elias's Crimson Dew without shaking. Progress!

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: SWEEP (of broom), CREAK (door closing)

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: I might be the only human working here, surrounded by creatures of the night. It's weird, it's dangerous, and honestly? It's kind of thrilling. Maybe one day I'll understand what's so 'fascinating' about my struggles. Until then, I'll just keep spilling coffee and trying not to get eaten.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: And maybe, just maybe, I'll figure out what Elias truly means when he looks at me like that.

summary: Eve, the only human barista at the vampire-run Nocturne Cafe, navigates a chaotic night shift, struggling with her clumsiness and the intimidating yet charming presence of a regular vampire patron, Elias.
